sections.content:
- Set up Doc Backlog database in Notion (31 tasks across cortex + pm_mvp)
- Scoped cortex v1: indexer + mcp-server only; viz and docjson-extraction are v1.1
- Audited all feature docs — 4 PRDs exist (indexer, mcp-server, viz, doc-manager), all but doc-manager are TODO stubs
- Identified 6 cortex v1 doc tasks: finish indexer PRD + 4 sub-feature PRDs (history, scanning, staleness, test-runner) + finish mcp-server PRD

Planned the viz overhaul across 4 PRDs. Created List View PRD (new), updated Graph View PRD, rewrote Detail Panel PRD, kept Staleness and Scanning PRDs engine-only.

**Branch:** `feature/viz-overhaul`

---

### Post-ADR-009 Alignment (2026-03-16)

ADR-009 (Unified Staleness and Node Types) was merged into master and rebased into this branch. Key changes that affect all remaining phases:

- **Staleness is persisted.** A `staleness TEXT` column on the `nodes` table is the single source of truth. `/api/all` reads it directly (no computation). `/api/check` recomputes via the shared engine in `cortex/index/staleness.py` and persists back.
- **Three divergent staleness implementations collapsed into one.** `_compute_staleness_from_history()`, `_compute_staleness_rich()` (both in viz server), and `_compute_staleness_for_nodes()` (MCP server) are all deleted. `_compute_staleness_for_viz()` is now a thin wrapper around the shared `compute_staleness()` engine.
- **Staleness vocabulary unified.** Six statuses, uniform across all node subtypes:
  - `CONTENT_STALE` — body/main content changed (replaces old `CODE_STALE`)
  - `DESC_STALE` — descriptor/heading changed
  - `LINKED_STALE` — a dependency changed via `documents` or `validates` edge (replaces old `CONTENT_STALE` for docs and `COVERAGE_STALE` for tests). NOT promotable.
  - `STRUCTURAL_DRIFT` — file or node no longer exists. NOT promotable.
  - `VERIFIED` — explicitly confirmed; both hash snapshots match
  - `CLEAN` — in sync
- **Node types reduced to three.** `composite_process`, `atomic_process`, `entity`. The `document` and `constraint` types are removed. Doc sections are `atomic_process` with `subtype=docjson`. Tests are `atomic_process` with a `test` tag.
- **Verification snapshots both hashes.** `node_verification` now stores `code_hash_at` AND `desc_hash_at`. A `desc_hash` change after verification is detected.
- **`build` computes staleness.** The build workflow's final step runs `compute_staleness()` and persists results, so the DB is always current after a build.

Phase 0 and Phase 1 code has been verified correct post-merge — endpoints already read from the persisted staleness column and bulk-verify no longer rejects doc sections.

---

**Phase 0 — Backend endpoints: DONE**

All 4 endpoints implemented in `cortex/viz/server.py`:
- `GET /api/nodes/{id}/staleness-cause` — reads persisted `staleness` column, enriches with current hashes (via AST re-parse for `CONTENT_STALE`/`DESC_STALE`), linked stale nodes (for `LINKED_STALE`), and verification info
- `POST /api/nodes/bulk-verify` — batch verification with per-node ok/error results. No node_type guard — all subtypes (including docjson) are verifiable.
- `GET /api/nodes/{id}/source` — source code with focused line range from `level_3_location`
- `GET /api/nodes/{id}/edges` — inbound/outbound edge list (single hop, no traversal)

Staleness data path: `cortex build` persists staleness → `/api/all` reads column directly → `/api/check` recomputes via shared engine when explicitly requested. Tests: 96 passed.

**Phase 1 — Detail panel tabs: DONE**

Refactored `detail.js` from single-scroll (313 lines) into 5-tab layout (~500 lines). Updated `app.js` selectNode() to fetch staleness-cause in parallel. Added CSS for tab bar, verify form, cause block, edge tables with dark mode overrides.

Tabs implemented:
- **Overview**: badges, ID+copy, location+copy, tags, level_0, staleness cause explanation (from persisted status + server-side enrichment), verify button with reason field, verification attribution
- **Docs**: level_1 ("Level 1 — Description"), level_2 ("Level 2 — Details"), empty state
- **API**: autodoc (params/returns/raises), docstring, merged steps (cortex + dFlow unified), "View Source" stub
- **Relationships**: test coverage, lazy-loaded inbound/outbound edge tables with jump links
- **History**: verification record, rename history, full change history (no 10-item cap)

Key decisions: tab state persists across node select

---

**Phase 2 — List view overhaul: DONE**

Rewrote `list.js` from 84 lines to ~520 lines across two sub-phases. Updated `index.html`, `app.js`, `style.css` with dark-mode overrides. One backend change: added `project_root` to `/api/meta` for VS Code URI construction.

**Phase 2a+2b — Structural + Actions (2026-03-16):**

- **Row grouping engine (US-LV-01):** Client-side grouping by type, subtype, module/file, staleness, or tag. Collapsible group headers with counts. Group-by radio selector in a new list-only sidebar section. Collapsed state persists across re-renders.
- **Clickable location column (US-LV-08):** Location text replaced with `vscode://file/…` links. Constructs absolute path from `project_root` (now exposed in `/api/meta`). Click opens file in VS Code at the correct line.
- **Dedicated list sidebar (US-LV-03):** New "Group By" and "Actions" sections appear only in list view. Existing shared filters remain shared. Sidebar sections toggle via `.list-only-section` class in `setView()`. No file fork — kept inline since TS migration is planned.
- **Staleness cause tooltip (US-LV-07):** Clicking a stale badge fetches `/api/nodes/{id}/staleness-cause` and shows a positioned tooltip with status, cause explanation, linked nodes, and verification info. Results cached per node. Tooltip auto-closes on outside click.
- **Verify per row (US-LV-04):** Checkmark button appears on hover for stale rows. Prompts for optional reason, POSTs to `/api/nodes/{id}/verify`, updates staleness map to VERIFIED and re-renders.
- **Bulk verify (US-LV-04):** Checkbox column with select-all header. Toolbar shows "N of M selected" count. "Verify Selected" button in sidebar POSTs to `/api/nodes/bulk-verify`. Clears selection on success, updates staleness summary.
- **Run Check from list (US-LV-05):** "Run Check" button in list sidebar triggers the same `App.check()` as the header button.

**Phase 2c — Advanced (2026-03-16):**

- **Code preview column (US-LV-02, Tier 1):** Optional "Code" column toggled via toolbar button or column menu. Shows 3-line monospace snippet from the node's focused source range, lazy-loaded from `/api/nodes/{id}/source` with client-side caching.
- **Source panel with Monaco (US-LV-02, Tier 2+3):** "View Source" link on code preview opens a bottom-split Monaco panel. Full file loaded in read-only `vs-dark` theme with minimap. Focus Function toggle switches between full file (with highlighted range) and isolated function view (offset line numbers). Drag-to-resize handle for panel height. Own Monaco instance, independent of Workflow/Tests.
- **Column show/hide (US-LV-09):** Dropdown column menu in toolbar with checkboxes for all 7 columns (select, name, type, location, status, tags, code). Column visibility persists across re-renders. Group header colspan adjusts dynamically.
- **Spawn graph from list (US-LV-06):** "View in Graph" button in sidebar. Uses selected node IDs (or all filtered IDs if none selected), filters `allEdges` to only connecting edges, loads into `Graph.loadAll()`, switches to graph view. Client-side only — no new backend endpoint needed.

No new backend endpoints in 2c — all data comes from existing Phase 0 endpoints. Tests: 119 passed.

Current priorities for the project. Updated the PRDs with AI assistance.

**Recommended order:**

**P0 — Scanner (do first, unblocks everything)**

1. `cortex init` / `cortex build` CLI split — small, high-visibility, sets the right mental model
2. AST call graph `validates` edges — the biggest piece of work, but it's the prerequisite for the test runner being useful at all

**P1 — Staleness surface (parallel with P0 if you want)**

3. Wire CONTENT_STALE into `cortex check` output — completely independent of the call graph work, can be done anytime

**P2 — Report (needs P0 + P1 to be meaningful)**

4. `cortex report` — once call graph edges exist and CONTENT_STALE is surfaced, the report has all three data sources (changed nodes, test results, stale doc sections)

**P3 — Gate (needs P2)**

5. GitHub action — trivial once `cortex report` and `cortex check --fail-on` both work

**P3 (anytime) — Rename detection**

6. Hash-similarity rename — improves data quality but nothing else depends on it; can slot in anywhere

sections.heading: 2026-03-16 — List View Polish
sections.content:
UX refinements to the Phase 2 list view based on hands-on usage feedback.

**Branch:** `feature/viz-overhaul` (continued)

---

**Layout change: 3-column (list | source | detail)**

The bottom-split source panel felt disconnected from the list. Moved to a right-side column layout: clicking a code node opens its source in a middle Monaco column, with the detail panel on the far right. Collapsible via toggle button. For docjson nodes, the source column renders section content as formatted HTML (headings + styled markdown) instead of raw JSON.

Removed the `{ } Code` toolbar button (confusing). "View Source" link now lives inline in the Name column for code nodes — always visible, no column toggle needed.

**Filtering improvements:**

- **Hide private functions by default.** Nodes with `_`-prefixed names are hidden. A "Show private" toggle in the sidebar reveals them. Reduces noise significantly for large codebases.
- **Test path filter from cortex.toml.** `/api/meta` now exposes `test_paths` (e.g. `["tests/"]`). The frontend uses this to filter out nodes whose location starts with a test path, independent of the `test` tag. Toggle: "Show tests".
- **Tags filter redesign.** Replaced the flat toggle pills with a docs-style filter panel: selected tag chips on top, searchable suggestion list below sorted by count, "Show all" overflow. Matches the doc manager UX.

**Location grouping: nested directory tree**

The flat file-path grouping was hard to parse. Replaced with a nested tree: directory rows are collapsible parent groups with indent scaled by depth, file rows are leaf groups containing the actual node rows. Only applies to location grouping — other modes stay flat.

Added **Collapse All / Expand All** toggle to the list toolbar (works for all grouping modes).

sections.heading: 2026-03-18 — History Feature Implementation Plan
sections.content:
History feature implementation plan. Covers the remaining history PRD backlog items across the indexer (Python) and viz (Python server + JS frontend).

**PRD**: `cortex::docs.features.indexer.sub_features.history.prd`
**Design**: `cortex::docs.features.indexer.sub_features.history.design`

---

### Config Scanner Added (2026-03-19)

Added `cortex/scanners/config_scanner.py` — scans `.claude/` and other config directories for settings, skills, and hook files. Produces `composite_process` nodes with `subtype=config`, whole-file hashing, mtime fast-pass.

**What changed:**
- New scanner: `config_scanner.py` with `scan_config_dir()` entry point
- `builder.py` step 5 calls config scanner for `.claude/` dirs
- `cortex_ontology.yaml`: added `config` as valid `composite_process` subtype
- `graph.js`: added `config: '#f0883e'` to `SUBTYPE_COLORS`
- Tests: 7 test functions in `tests/test_config_scanner.py`

**Docs updated:**
- Architecture, MVP, data model (markdown docs)
- Indexer design, viz design, viz PRD (DocJSON docs)
- This devlog entry
